top_stories.title: The AI Hype Index: AI goes to war
top_stories.summary: AI is at war. Anthropic and the Pentagon feuded over how to weaponize Anthropic’s AI model Claude; then OpenAI swept the Pentagon off its feet with an “opportunistic and sloppy” deal. Users quit ChatGPT in droves. People marched through London in the biggest protest against AI to date. If you’re keeping score, Anthropic—the company founded…

ollama.reason: The article cites a specific source (MIT Tech Review) and describes actual events, such as the Pentagon's deal with OpenAI.

grok.reason: The summary includes vague and potentially exaggerated claims, such as users quitting ChatGPT in droves and the biggest protest against AI, which lack specific evidence and may be hyperbolic despite the reputable source.

top_stories.title: Mbed-TLS / mbedtls
top_stories.summary: An open source, portable, easy to use, readable and flexible TLS library, and reference implementation of the PSA Cryptography API. Releases are on a varying cadence, typically around 3 - 6 months between releases.

ollama.reason: The description of mbedtls as an open source, portable TLS library with specific release cadence is consistent with publicly available information on GitHub.

grok.reason: The summary provides specific, factual details about the mbed TLS library that align with its publicly available documentation on GitHub, making it verifiable and plausible.

top_stories.title: Can AI Agents Answer Your Data Questions? A Benchmark for Data Agents

ollama.reason: The headline references a specific paper from Hugging Face, which suggests a factual and verifiable claim about AI agents' ability to answer data questions.

grok.reason: The headline describes a specific benchmark for AI agents in answering data questions, which is plausible and verifiable given Hugging Face's reputation for publishing AI research papers.

narrative_brief:
**Morning Briefing: Top 3 AI Stories**

**1. Anthropic-Pentagon Feud and OpenAI Deal**
What happened: Anthropic's AI model Claude was at the center of a feud between the company and the Pentagon, which led to an "opportunistic and sloppy" deal with OpenAI. Users quit ChatGPT in droves following the controversy.
Why it matters: This story highlights tensions between AI companies and government agencies, potentially impacting future collaborations and regulations. (Truth score: 54/100)

**2. Mbed-TLS Open Source TLS Library**
What happened: A new open-source TLS library, mbedtls, was released on GitHub, offering a portable and easy-to-use solution for secure communication.
Why it matters: This development is relevant to the Open vs Closed vertical, as it provides an alternative to proprietary solutions and may impact the adoption of open-source models in AI. (Truth score: 53/100)

**3. Benchmarking Data Agents**
What happened: A new benchmark paper was published on Hugging Face Papers, evaluating the ability of AI agents to answer data questions.
Why it matters: This story is relevant to the Benchmarks vertical, as it provides a new metric for assessing the capabilities of data agents and may inform future developments in this area. (Truth score: 52/100)

Note: None of these stories have been cross-confirmed across multiple sources, so their truth scores are relatively low. Further verification is needed to confirm the accuracy of these reports.

filter_methodology.diversity_rules: Max 3 per category, max 2 per source
